card.intuition: An architectural philosophy by Robert C. Martin (Uncle Bob) that organizes code into concentric layers, ensuring dependency only flows inward toward the core 'Business Rules'.
card.analogy: An onion: the 'Business Logic' (Entities / Use Cases) is the core. The 'Databases' and 'UI' are the outer skins. You can peel off the outer skin (switch from Web to Mobile, or SQL to MongoDB) without hurting the core of the onion.
when_to_use: Large-scale enterprise systems with complex domain logic
Projects that need to be completely independent of their Database or Framework
Teams that want to test their business logic in isolation from the UI
gotchas: Extreme Boilerplate: preparing one data request requires passing an object through 4 layers of 'Controllers', 'Interactors', 'Gateways', and 'Entities'.
Over-engineering for small apps: it adds a massive amount of overhead for simple CRUD operations.

steps.annotation: System overview — step through to trace the flow.
Outer ring: frameworks and I/O. Most volatile — can be swapped.
Adapters translate between use-case format and external format.
Use cases contain application-specific business rules.
Entities: core business logic, no dependencies. Most stable ring.

exercise.prompt: Identify the primary rule of Clean Architecture regarding dependencies.
hints: Toward the center of the onion
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "inward"
assert check("inward")
print("PASS")
